design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore side-scrolling levels collecting a hidden number of keys, ride captured animals with distinct abilities to overcome obstacles, reach exit with sufficient keys
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource discovery (keys hidden throughout large levels, quantity unknown until reaching exit) combined with execution challenge and animal power selection
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Inhabit a child's surreal dream world, ride fantastical creatures with unique powers to navigate obstacle-filled journey to rescue the King of Slumberland
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game refuses to tell player how many keys are needed before exit encounter—forces exploration and backtracking; animal riding mechanic ties progression to puzzle-solving (which animal ability is needed here?) rather than pure platforming skill; intentionally punishing difficulty as design statement rather than accident

difficulty.lives_system: Standard lives system with Game Over state
difficulty.checkpointing: Checkpoint-less within levels; death restarts from level beginning
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Hidden level select (cheat code: Up, Select, Left, Right, A, A, B at title) allows skipping ahead; animal selection provides variable difficulty within levels—some animals grant extra health or necessary abilities, others merely ease progression

progression.upgrade_system: Animal companion system—feed candy to animals to unlock their riding ability; each animal has distinct moveset (frog, gorilla, mole, etc.) with unique traversal/combat properties
progression.unlock_mechanism: Levels unlock sequentially; level select cheat available for skilled/determined players
replayability_hooks: Multiple animal routes through same level offering different puzzle solutions
Key-hunting encourages thorough exploration of large levels
Difficulty progression noted as 'well thought-out' by contemporary critics
Regional differences (Manta Ray boss pattern varies JP vs US/EU) suggest content iteration

world_building_techniques: Thematic level design reflecting dream logic (surreal, non-Euclidean spaces implied by vertical/horizontal traversal)
Character-driven progression toward specific goal (rescue Morpheus from Nightmare King)
Pastel/cartoon aesthetic reinforces storybook dream world atmosphere
Animal companions populate dreamscape with personality (smoking gorilla in JP version, removed in US/EU for content standards)

boss_design.philosophy: Pattern-based encounters with regional tuning; Manta Ray boss received pattern adjustment between JP and US/EU versions suggesting iterative difficulty balancing

danger_signals: Instant death traps noted by contemporary critics
Collision detection issues creating unfair deaths
Sprite flickering under load reducing visual clarity for hazard avoidance
'Punishingly difficult' nature acts as gating mechanism for casual players
environmental_storytelling: Dreamscape level design with surreal geometry
Animal presence in each level creates implicit puzzle: 'which creature ability do I need here?'
Candy-feeding mechanic ties player agency to environmental world-building
Key scattering throughout large levels tells story of exploration-focused design philosophy
